content.title: ISC2 Certified in Cybersecurity (CC)
content.summary: ISC2 Certified in Cybersecurity (CC) is a foundational, vendor-neutral cybersecurity certification for newcomers and career switchers. It focuses on core principles, governance, access control, network security, and operations rather than deep tool specialization.

credential_details.intended_audience: newcomers, career switchers, and early-career security candidates

knowledge_areas: Security principles: CIA triad, defense in depth, shared responsibility, and security controls.
Business continuity, disaster recovery, and incident response concepts.
Access-control concepts: authentication, authorization, IAM, least privilege, and common access models.
Network security fundamentals: components, secure protocols, segmentation, and common threats.
Security operations: logging, monitoring, change management, asset protection, and physical security.
learning_outcomes: Apply core cybersecurity principles using accurate professional terminology.
Understand how organizations manage risk, access control, resilience, and security operations.
Distinguish authentication, authorization, and common access-control models.
Describe incident response, business continuity, and disaster recovery clearly.
Build a foundation for more advanced certifications such as SSCP or CISSP.
tools_and_frameworks: Core frameworks and concepts: CIA triad, defense in depth, least privilege, IAM, incident response lifecycle, resilience concepts, and governance-aligned security practice.
Exposure type: concept-heavy and tool-agnostic, intended to prepare learners for later hands-on operational work.

proof.label: Credly badge: ISC2 Certified in Cybersecurity (CC)
